B Manish Reddy (AM.EN.U4AIE22014) Midhun S (AM.EN.U4AIE22032) Sreeram R (AM.EN.U4AIE22047)

AMRITA VISHWA VIDYAPEETHAM May 14, 2025

AI-EMS SRS 4 of 16

## Revision History

| Name           | Date         | Reason for Changes | Version |
|----------------|--------------|--------------------|---------|
| B Manish Reddy | May 14, 2025 | Initial draft      | 1.0     |

# 1 Introduction

#### 1.1 Purpose

This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) provides a comprehensive description of the AI-Enhanced Employee Management System (AI-EMS), an advanced HR management solution that integrates artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) to optimize workforce management. The document details functional and non-functional requirements, system interfaces, and constraints for developers, stakeholders, and users, ensuring a clear roadmap for development.

#### 1.2 Document Conventions

- Prioritization: Requirements are prioritized as High, Medium, or Low, with functional requirements inheriting the priority of their parent feature.
- Formatting: Bold text denotes section headings, and bulleted lists highlight key points. Requirements are numbered (e.g., FR-1, NFR-1) for reference.
- Section Numbering: Hierarchical numbering (e.g., 1.1, 3.2.1) ensures structured navigation.
- Font: Consistent typeface for readability, aligned with mentor-approved standards.

## 1.3 Intended Audience and Reading Suggestions

- Developers: Focus on Sections 3 (System Features), 4 (External Interface Requirements), and 5 (Non-Functional Requirements) for technical specifications.
- Project Managers: Review Sections 1 (Introduction) and 2 (Overall Description) for project scope and objectives.
- Testers: Refer to Section 3 for functional requirements and Section 5 for performance and reliability criteria.
- Users (HR Managers, Employees): Read Section 2.2 (Product Features) and 2.3 (User Classes) for system functionality.
- Documentation Writers: Use Sections 1.2 (Document Conventions) and 2.6 (User Documentation) for guidance.
- AI Researchers/Students: Focus on Sections 3.8–3.11 for AI/ML features.

### 1.4 Project Scope

The AI-EMS enhances traditional employee management systems by integrating AI and ML to automate HR processes, provide intelligent insights, and improve employee satisfaction. Key objectives include:

• Automating routine HR tasks (e.g., attendance, leave, payroll).

AI-EMS SRS 5 of 16

- Providing AI-driven predictions for performance, churn, and task optimization.
- Offering a user-friendly interface with an AI chatbot for employee support.
- Ensuring compliance with data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA).
- Serving as a learning platform for AI undergraduate students.

The system benefits HR managers, department managers, employees, and system administrators by streamlining operations, reducing costs, and fostering data-driven decision-making.

## 1.5 References

- 1. Base Employee Management System [Github Repository](https://github.com/kiranshahi/Employee-Management-System)
- 2. Laravel Documentation <https://laravel.com/docs>
- 3. MySQL Documentation <https://dev.mysql.com/doc/>
- 4. Bootstrap Documentation <https://getbootstrap.com/docs/>
- 5. Scikit-learn Documentation <https://scikit-learn.org/stable/documentation.html>
- 6. TensorFlow Documentation <https://www.tensorflow.org/guide>
- 7. PyTorch Documentation <https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/index.html>
- 8. Sommerville, I. (2015). Software Engineering (10th ed.). Pearson.

## 2 Overall Description

## 2.1 Product Perspective

The AI-EMS is a standalone web application extending traditional employee management systems with AI/ML capabilities. Built using Laravel (backend), MySQL (database), Bootstrap (frontend), and Python-based AI/ML libraries (Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch), it integrates with existing HR workflows while offering advanced features like sentiment analysis and churn prediction. The system can connect to third-party services via RESTful APIs, enhancing its versatility.

## 2.2 Product Features

- Employee Management: CRUD operations for employee records, including personal details, skills, and documents.
- Attendance Tracking: Records check-in/check-out times with AI-based pattern prediction.
- Leave Management: Manages leave requests, approvals, and balances with AI predictions.
- Task Management: Assigns and tracks tasks with AI-optimized assignments.
- Performance Evaluation: Conducts evaluations with AI-driven performance predictions.
- Payroll Management: Calculates salaries and detects anomalies using AI.
- AI Chatbot: Provides instant employee support via NLP.

AI-EMS SRS 6 of 16

- Sentiment Analysis: Analyzes employee feedback for sentiment trends.
- Employee Churn Prediction: Identifies and mitigates churn risks.
- AI Recruitment: Ranks candidates and streamlines hiring.

## 2.3 User Classes and Characteristics

- Admin/HR Manager: Full system access; manages employee data and uses AI insights for optimization. Requires robust UI and reporting tools.
- Department Managers: Manages team tasks and performance; needs AI-driven team insights. Requires task and evaluation interfaces.
- Employees: Views personal data, applies for leave, and interacts with the chatbot. Needs intuitive UI.
- System Administrators: Maintains system, manages accounts, and ensures security. Requires admin tools and logs.
- AI Researchers/Students: Accesses AI models and datasets for learning. Requires API documentation and model details.

## 2.4 Operating Environment

- Web Browsers: Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Safari, Microsoft Edge (latest versions).
- Server: Apache or Nginx.
- Database: MySQL 8.0+.
- Backend: Laravel (PHP 8.1+).
- Frontend: Bootstrap 5.
- AI/ML: Python 3.9+ with Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch.
- Operating Systems: Windows, macOS, Linux.
- Devices: Desktops, laptops, tablets, smartphones (responsive design).

## 2.5 Design and Implementation Constraints

- Must integrate with existing employee management system structures.
- AI models must run efficiently on standard server hardware.
- Must comply with GDPR, CCPA, and other data privacy regulations.
- Must scale to handle growing employee records and concurrent users.
- Third-party integrations require secure, documented APIs.

AI-EMS SRS 7 of 16

## 2.6 User Documentation

- User manuals for each user class (Admin, Manager, Employee).
- Online help system with tooltips and FAQs.
- Administrator guide for system setup and maintenance.
- Installation and configuration guide for deployment.
- API documentation for integration.
- AI model documentation for training and evaluation.

## 2.7 Assumptions and Dependencies

- Users have basic computer literacy and access to supported browsers.
- Historical employee data is available for AI model training.
- Continuous internet access is available for cloud-based AI services.
- Server environment supports Laravel and Python integration.
- Third-party APIs (e.g., email services) remain stable.

# 3 System Features

## 3.1 User Authentication and Authorization

### 3.1.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Secure login and role-based access control for all users.
- Priority: High.

### 3.1.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: User enters credentials on login page.
- Response: System validates credentials and grants access based on role.

#### 3.1.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-1: System shall provide secure login with username and password.
- FR-2: System shall enforce role-based access control (e.g., Admin, Employee).
- FR-3: Admins shall create, modify, and delete user accounts.
- FR-4: System shall enforce password policies (min. 8 characters, mixed case, numbers).
- FR-5: System shall provide password reset via email.

AI-EMS SRS 8 of 16

## 3.2 Employee Management

#### 3.2.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Manages employee records, including personal and job details.
- Priority: High.

## 3.2.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: Admin adds a new employee via form.
- Response: System saves employee data and displays confirmation.

## 3.2.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-6: Admins shall add, view, edit, and delete employee information.
- FR-7: System shall store employee details (name, contact, job role, documents).
- FR-8: System shall support employee profile pictures.
- FR-9: Employees shall update personal information (pending approval).
- FR-10: System shall provide search and filter for employee records.

## 3.3 Attendance Management

## 3.3.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Tracks employee attendance with AI predictions.
- Priority: High.

#### 3.3.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: Employee checks in via interface.
- Response: System records check-in time and updates attendance.

#### 3.3.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-11: System shall track check-in/check-out times.
- FR-12: System shall generate attendance reports.
- FR-13: Admins shall edit attendance records manually.
- FR-14: System shall notify for attendance anomalies.
- FR-15: System shall predict attendance patterns using AI.

# 3.4 Leave Management

#### 3.4.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Manages leave requests and balances with AI predictions.
- Priority: High.

AI-EMS SRS 9 of 16

### 3.4.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: Employee submits leave request.
- Response: System notifies manager for approval.

### 3.4.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-16: Employees shall apply for leave.
- FR-17: Managers shall approve/reject leave requests.
- FR-18: System shall track leave balances.
- FR-19: System shall generate leave reports.
- FR-20: System shall predict leave patterns using AI.

# 3.5 Task Management

## 3.5.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Assigns and tracks tasks with AI optimization.
- Priority: Medium.

## 3.5.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: Manager assigns task to employee.
- Response: System notifies employee and tracks task progress.

### 3.5.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-21: Managers shall create and assign tasks.
- FR-22: Employees shall view and update assigned tasks.
- FR-23: System shall track task status and completion.
- FR-24: System shall generate task reports.
- FR-25: System shall optimize task assignments using AI.

# 3.6 Performance Evaluation

### 3.6.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Conducts performance evaluations with AI predictions.
- Priority: Medium.

#### 3.6.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: Manager submits evaluation form.
- Response: System saves evaluation and updates employee record.

AI-EMS SRS 10 of 16

#### 3.6.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-26: Managers shall conduct performance evaluations.
- FR-27: System shall store historical performance data.
- FR-28: System shall generate performance reports.
- FR-29: System shall predict employee performance using AI.
- FR-30: System shall recommend performance improvements.

# 3.7 Payroll Management

## 3.7.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Manages salaries with AI anomaly detection.
- Priority: High.

## 3.7.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: Admin generates payroll for a period.
- Response: System calculates salaries and generates payslips.

### 3.7.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-31: System shall calculate salaries based on attendance and leave.
- FR-32: System shall generate payroll reports.
- FR-33: System shall track salary history.
- FR-34: System shall detect payroll anomalies using AI.
- FR-35: System shall provide salary forecasting.

# 3.8 AI Chatbot

#### 3.8.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Provides instant employee support via NLP.
- Priority: Medium.

#### 3.8.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: Employee sends query to chatbot.
- Response: Chatbot responds or escalates to HR.

AI-EMS SRS 11 of 16

#### 3.8.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-36: System shall provide an AI chatbot for queries.
- FR-37: Chatbot shall respond to common HR queries instantly.
- FR-38: Chatbot shall escalate complex queries to HR.
- FR-39: Chatbot shall learn from interactions.
- FR-40: Chatbot shall use NLP for query understanding.

## 3.9 Sentiment Analysis

## 3.9.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Analyzes employee feedback for sentiment trends.
- Priority: Medium.

## 3.9.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: Employee submits feedback.
- Response: System analyzes sentiment and generates report.

### 3.9.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-41: System shall analyze feedback for sentiment.
- FR-42: System shall generate sentiment reports.
- FR-43: System shall alert managers to negative sentiment.
- FR-44: System shall recommend satisfaction improvements.
- FR-45: System shall track sentiment trends.

# 3.10 Employee Churn Prediction

#### 3.10.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Predicts employee churn risks.
- Priority: Medium.

#### 3.10.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: System analyzes employee data.
- Response: System identifies high-risk employees and factors.

AI-EMS SRS 12 of 16

#### 3.10.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-46: System shall predict employee churn likelihood.
- FR-47: System shall identify churn factors.
- FR-48: System shall recommend retention strategies.
- FR-49: System shall generate churn prediction reports.
- FR-50: System shall alert managers to high-risk employees.

### 3.11 AI Recruitment Module

#### 3.11.1 Description and Priority

- Description: Streamlines hiring with AI candidate ranking.
- Priority: Medium.

#### 3.11.2 Stimulus/Response Sequences

- Stimulus: HR posts job opening.
- Response: System ranks candidates based on job fit.

#### 3.11.3 Functional Requirements

- FR-51: HR shall post job openings.
- FR-52: System shall track candidate applications.
- FR-53: System shall rank candidates using AI.
- FR-54: System shall manage interview scheduling.
- FR-55: System shall generate recruitment reports.

# 4 External Interface Requirements

## 4.1 User Interfaces

- Login Interface: Secure form with username/password fields.
- Dashboard: Role-based dashboard with metrics and quick links.
- Employee Management: Forms for CRUD operations, search/filter options.
- Attendance Management: Calendar view for check-in/check-out.
- Leave Management: Forms for leave applications and approvals.
- Task Management: Task board with drag-and-drop functionality.
- Performance Evaluation: Evaluation forms and history view.
- Payroll Management: Salary details and payslip generation.

AI-EMS SRS 13 of 16

- AI Insights: Visualizations for predictions and trends.
- AI Chatbot: Chat window with quick response buttons.
- Recruitment: Job posting and candidate management forms.

#### 4.2 Hardware Interfaces

- Server Hardware: Standard server with Apache/Nginx.
- Client Hardware: Desktops, laptops, tablets, smartphones with browsers.
- Biometric Devices (Optional): Fingerprint scanners for attendance.

#### 4.3 Software Interfaces

- Database: MySQL 8.0+ for data storage/retrieval.
- Web Server: Apache/Nginx for hosting.
- AI/ML: Python with Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch for model training/inference.
- API: RESTful API for third-party integration.
- Notification: SMTP for emails, WebSockets for real-time updates.

### 4.4 Communications Interfaces

- HTTP/HTTPS: Secure web communication.
- SMTP: Email notifications for approvals and alerts.
- WebSockets: Real-time chatbot and notifications.

# 5 Other Non-Functional Requirements

## 5.1 Performance Requirements

- NFR-1: System shall support 1000 concurrent users.
- NFR-2: User requests shall respond within 2 seconds.
- NFR-3: System shall handle 10,000 employee records.
- NFR-4: AI model inference shall complete within 5 seconds.
- NFR-5: System shall process 100 transactions/second.

AI-EMS SRS 14 of 16

### 5.2 Security Requirements

- NFR-6: All sensitive data shall be encrypted (TLS/SSL).
- NFR-7: Multi-factor authentication for admin access.
- NFR-8: System shall maintain audit trails for critical actions.
- NFR-9: System shall comply with GDPR and CCPA.
- NFR-10: Role-based access control for all users.

### 5.3 Reliability Requirements

- NFR-11: System uptime shall be 99.9%.
- NFR-12: Daily automatic data backups.
- NFR-13: Fault tolerance mechanisms for high availability.
- NFR-14: Error handling and recovery for all operations.
- NFR-15: Data integrity during concurrent operations.

## 5.4 Usability Requirements

- NFR-16: Responsive design for all screen sizes.
- NFR-17: Consistent navigation and UI across modules.
- NFR-18: Clear, helpful error messages.
- NFR-19: Support for keyboard navigation.
- NFR-20: Tooltips and help documentation for users.

# 5.5 Scalability Requirements

- NFR-21: Scalable for growing user base.
- NFR-22: Scalable for increasing data volume.
- NFR-23: Support horizontal scaling.
- NFR-24: Caching for performance optimization.
- NFR-25: Optimized database queries for large datasets.

# 5.6 Maintainability Requirements

- NFR-26: Modular design for easy updates.
- NFR-27: Well-documented code with comments.
- NFR-28: Logging for debugging and monitoring.
- NFR-29: Designed for ease of maintenance.
- NFR-30: Adherence to coding standards (PSR, PEP 8).

AI-EMS SRS 15 of 16

#### 5.7 Compatibility Requirements

- NFR-31: Compatible with Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge.
- NFR-32: Compatible with mobile devices.
- NFR-33: Compatible with Windows, macOS, Linux.
- NFR-34: API compatibility for integration.
- NFR-35: Support for data import/export (CSV, JSON).

## 6 Other Requirements

• None at this time.

# Appendices

### Appendix A: Glossary

- AI-EMS: AI-Enhanced Employee Management System.
- HR: Human Resources.
- ML: Machine Learning.
- UI: User Interface.
- API: Application Programming Interface.
- DBMS: Database Management System.
- CRUD: Create, Read, Update, Delete.
- NLP: Natural Language Processing.
- SRS: Software Requirements Specification.

## Appendix B: Analysis Models

- Use Case Diagram: Illustrates interactions between actors (Admin, Manager, Employee) and system functions (e.g., manage employee, predict churn).
- Activity Diagram: Depicts workflow for AI-powered performance prediction, from data collection to result presentation.
- Data Flow Diagram (DFD): Shows data flow for key processes like sentiment analysis and task assignment.

AI-EMS SRS 16 of 16

## Appendix C: Issues List

## • Open Issues:

– Intermittent time-zone discrepancies in attendance logs leading to incorrect check-in/out times.

- Occasional AI prediction drift due to outdated model weights between scheduled retraining.
- Email/SMS notification delays under high load, causing late alerts to employees.
- UI alignment glitch on mobile screens for the Task Management dashboard.

## • Resolved Issues:

- Fixed OAuth token refresh bug that caused user sessions to expire unexpectedly (Issue #42).
- Patched SQL injection vulnerability in the leave-application endpoint (Issue #37).
- Optimized database indexing on the performance metrics tables, reducing report generation time by 60%.
- Eliminated memory leak in the AI model serving container through improved resource cleanup (Issue #45).

## • Future Enhancements:

- Multilingual support for global accessibility.
- Integration with wearable devices for automated attendance tracking.
- Advanced AI module for real-time employee wellness monitoring and sentiment analysis.
- Role-based dashboard customization allowing managers to design bespoke widgets.
- Offline mode with local caching for field-based employees in low-connectivity areas.
